chapter_title.en: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings

text: 517 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ أُصِيبَ بِمُصِيبَةٍ جَزِعَ عَلَيْهَا أَوْ لَمْ يَجْزَعْ صَبَرَ عَلَيْهَا أَمْ لَمْ يَصْبِرْ كَانَ ثَوَابُهُ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اَلْجَنَّةَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.517 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever is afflicted with a calamity, whether they express intense grief over it or not, whether they exercise patience or not, their reward from Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, is Paradise."
